skipped.component-design: Deferred to the design-analysis co-design stop, where the component map and flow must be built with the human before plan.md.

agenda: Decide the exact specrew start versus SessionStart hook division of labor.
Decide the B2 full-bootstrap versus lightweight welcome-back trigger.
Keep F-171 dispatcher behavior stable while adding a bootstrap provider.
architecture-core.decision: Human-confirmed (facilitated one decision at a time, 2026-06-08): (1) division of labor = launcher preface + hook bootstrap, hook is the only full orientation/menu source; (2) B2 classification is handover-first and two-stage - read+validate handover against current project state before anchor classification, recency necessary but not sufficient; (3) concurrency posture is advisory-only, no locks/lease, cross-machine prevention out of scope; (4) IDesign volatility-based decomposition (volatile host adapters over stable classification/validation/clearing/directive engines). Out of scope: B1/B3 unchanged, B4 + Antigravity deferred, distributed same-feature prevention deferred. See workshop/architecture-core.md.
architecture-core.diagram: workshop/architecture-core.md - confirmed posture, two-stage handover-first classification, advisory concurrency, volatility decomposition

agenda: Define SessionStart event input to bootstrap directive output contract.
Define SessionEnd handover-write integration with Proposal 130.
Empirically verify host-specific menu rendering across Claude, Codex, Copilot, and Cursor.
integration-api.decision: Human-confirmed (facilitated one decision at a time, 2026-06-08): (1) contract ownership - F-171 owns event ingestion/breaker/dedupe/journal, Proposal 130 owns handover format, Feature 174 owns the data-oriented bootstrap directive (fields: mode, sources, required_reads, render_first, menu_intent, validation_findings, dedupe/session metadata) and classification rules; (1b/2) add an advisory SessionStart marker via the hook journal - no git commit, no handover rewrite on startup - to detect unclean prior exits; (3) error handling is fail-open to safe bootstrap, never hard-block, invalid state never authoritative resume; (4) verification = unit/contract + handover round-trip fixtures + B1/B3 regression + per-host empirical evidence (render-before-picker) for Claude/Codex/Copilot/Cursor. See workshop/integration-api.md.
integration-api.diagram: workshop/integration-api.md - confirmed contract ownership, data-oriented directive, SessionStart marker, fail-open error envelope, host verification baseline

agenda: Define visible orientation plus Resume/New/Pick menu sequence.
Resolve structured-picker versus prose-only fallback per host.
Preserve render-before-menu discipline from F-165.
ui-ux.decision: Human-confirmed (facilitated one decision at a time, 2026-06-08): (1) render sequence = strict render-first (orientation, validated handover summary, state line, text menu) BEFORE any picker, enforced mechanically via a disallowed-tools:AskUserQuestion skill (gate-stop-style) because hook directive text is advisory-only and collapses on Claude - makes FR-004/SC-001 enforced not hoped-for; (2) prose-first floor on every host + per-host structured-picker opt-in gated on FR-005 empirical evidence; (3) self-explaining states - always show the mode line, plus a one-line reason from validation_findings only when something non-default happened (cleared anchor, historical handover, unclean exit, mismatch). See workshop/ui-ux.md.
ui-ux.diagram: workshop/ui-ux.md - confirmed render-first sequence + mechanical enforcement, prose floor + gated per-host picker, self-explaining states

agenda: Classify session anchors, handover records, and index metadata.
Define validity and freshness checks before resume candidates are surfaced.
Treat absolute worktree path anchors as non-portable.
data-storage.decision: Human-confirmed (facilitated one decision at a time, 2026-06-08): (1) exit hook is write-only - writes the Proposal 130 handover on clean exit, no git ops by default (no -A, no commit, no push); live anchor/marker/active-session signals are local-only and advisory; residual committed state is non-authoritative; interactive commit-on-exit is unsupported (FR-003) so the commit/push-to-continue-elsewhere choice is surfaced at the next bootstrap, with an off-by-default opt-in scoped-commit flag; (2) staleness is event/state-first (handover validity via commit reachability + open/closed + portability + artifact match, Proposal 130 wall-clock only a hint), single configurable time window only for the active-session marker; (3) feature resolution re-resolves the ref to the current project root (never the absolute path, FR-015), judges resumability from project-local presence + active-features registry + artifact status with git merged-status corroboration, clears the anchor on failure (FR-013). See workshop/data-storage.md.
data-storage.diagram: workshop/data-storage.md - write-only exit hook, event/state-first staleness, project-local resolution with git corroboration

agenda: Identify trust boundaries for hook event JSON, filesystem state, absolute paths, and generated directives.
Define failure-safe behavior for stale, untrusted, or defunct-worktree anchors.
Prevent hook interactivity or external state from steering unauthorized lifecycle continuation.
security-compliance.decision: Human delegated intake decision: security-compliance applies at medium depth because stale anchors and absolute paths can misdirect session recovery and the hook must stay non-interactive and failure-safe.

agenda: Define journal, breaker, dedupe, and state-classification evidence for B2 bootstrap paths.
Ensure full bootstrap, light welcome-back, and cleared-anchor behavior are diagnosable.
Define failure evidence for missing or invalid handover/session-state files.
observability-resilience.decision: Human delegated intake decision: observability-resilience applies at medium depth because the feature changes startup behavior and must be reviewable through logs, tests, and hook journal evidence.

agenda: Set compatibility and idempotency as design-driving NFRs.
Preserve unchanged B1 and B3 behavior as regression criteria.
Keep B4 pre-compaction capture and Antigravity binding out of scope.
requirements-nfr.decision: Human delegated intake decision: requirements-nfr applies at medium depth because the feature is mostly behavioral posture and compatibility, with measurable outcomes needed for per-host reliability and unchanged-scope claims.

agenda: Define deployment-loop impact for B2 bootstrap provider and SessionEnd handover registration.
Preserve kill-switch and managed-marker behavior from F-171.
Avoid introducing a new install path.
devops-operations.decision: Human delegated intake decision: devops-operations applies at light depth because the feature changes host hook registration and rollout controls, but it should reuse the shipped F-171 deployment loop.
